descripcion: "Mi Ultimo Adiós" by José Rizal is a poem written on the eve of his execution. Originally untitled, this work became the most celebrated poem related to martyrdom in modern history. Written in Fort Santiago before his death, Rizal expressed his love for his homeland despite facing capital punishment imposed by Spanish authorities and friars who opposed his calls for reform. The poem has been translated into over 80 languages worldwide, reflecting its enduring impact. (This is an automatically generated summary.)
